commands.hypothesis: Loosened version of v36-conviction-buyers (which fired 0 trades in 36h). Cohort B Panel 2: whale_pct d=+0.224, unique_buyers d=-0.280. Untraded panel: both clean (|d|<0.15). Only 2 existing strategies reference these fields. Captures 'few large conviction buyers' pattern — real demand concentrated in whales, not coordinated sniper bots. Thresholds set from cohort B medians: whale_pct >= 0.25 (winner median 0.296, loser 0.236) and unique_buyers <= 15 (winner median 12, loser 18, loosened from 5 for fill rate). TP/SL=50/30 from best-combos empirical consensus.

Creator reputation filter on prior rug rate. Cohort B Panel 2: d=-0.201, with striking quartile gap — Bot-Q WR (creators with lowest rug rates) = 96.3% winner vs Top-Q WR (highest rug rates) = 55.6%. Panel 5: untraded d=-0.105 (very clean — no existing roster bias). Only 1 existing strategy uses this column. Threshold: prior_rug_rate < 0.30 (winner median 0.25, loser 0.37). Sample sizes smaller than other v37 candidates (winner_n=67, loser_n=42) but n>30 per side so not low-conf. TP/SL=50/30.

Pure flow-sells filter — zero existing strategies use flow_sol_sells_0_30, so this is a truly novel signal. Cohort B Panel 2: d=-0.223 (lower sells = winner). Quartile gap: Bot-Q WR (lowest sells) = 62.4% winner vs Top-Q WR (highest sells) = 40.9%. Panel 5: untraded d very low (<0.10 — completely clean). Threshold: flow_sol_sells_0_30 < 5 SOL (winner median 3.95, loser 6.06; slightly above winner median to balance signal vs fill rate). Captures 'tokens where dumpers aren't piling on in the first 30s' which is intuitively the early-rug filter the bot doesn't currently have. TP/SL=50/30.
